summary:
The patient with congenital hyperthyroidism usually experiences earlier dental eruptions instead of delays; this makes option B stand apart from others associated with delayed teeth emergence such as hypothyroidism or nutritional deficiencies causing rickets. \boxed{B}

The summary captures only two core aspects necessary for answering the given question accurately while adhering strictly within the specified length constraint by focusing solely on critical information pertinent directly related to distinguishing between options provided concerning their effect upon timing of teething processes. It highlights why one condition does not fit based primarily around its physiological opposite action relative to typical developmental timelines observed clinically across various endocrine states impacting skeletal growth patterns including those influencing jawbone formation where permanent incisors first appear during childhood years after birth.
